name: Naturepedia Water System Map
description: Machine-readable hydrological system map connecting precipitation, runoff, infiltration, groundwater recharge, rivers, wetlands, floodplains, estuaries, coastal systems, watersheds, and weather constraints.

about.name: Precipitation
about.description: Atmospheric water delivered to the surface as rain, snow, sleet, or other forms.

about.name: Surface Runoff
about.description: Water moving across land toward streams, rivers, wetlands, floodplains, lakes, and coastal systems.

about.name: Infiltration
about.description: Movement of surface water into soil and subsurface layers.

about.name: Groundwater Recharge
about.description: Downward movement of infiltrated water that replenishes groundwater systems and aquifers.

about.name: Springs and River Baseflow
about.description: Groundwater discharge that sustains springs, streams, and river flow between precipitation events.

about.name: Wetland Storage
about.description: Temporary or seasonal storage of water that influences filtration, flood moderation, habitat, and nutrient behavior.

about.name: Floodplain Inundation
about.description: Expansion of river water across adjacent floodplains during elevated discharge.

about.name: Groundwater–Estuary Interface
about.description: Subsurface freshwater and nutrient exchange between groundwater systems and estuaries.

about.name: River–Estuary–Coastal Interface
about.description: Interaction among river discharge, tides, salinity, sediment transport, and coastal processes.

about.name: Weather Constraints
about.description: Precipitation, snowpack, drought, storms, temperature, and seasonal atmospheric conditions that regulate hydrological behavior.

about.name: Chesapeake Bay
about.description: A major estuarine system demonstrating watershed, river, wetland, tidal, sediment, and coastal relationships.

about.name: Yellowstone Watershed
about.description: A watershed example connecting snowpack, seasonal melt, groundwater, rivers, wetlands, wildlife habitat, and downstream flow.

hasPart.name: Precipitation to River Pathway
hasPart.description: Precipitation → surface runoff → river discharge → floodplain inundation.

hasPart.name: Recharge to Baseflow Pathway
hasPart.description: Precipitation → infiltration → groundwater recharge → springs and river baseflow.

hasPart.name: Wetland Storage Pathway
hasPart.description: River discharge → wetland storage → filtration, flood moderation, and habitat response.

hasPart.name: Groundwater to Estuary Pathway
hasPart.description: Groundwater discharge → estuarine freshwater and nutrient input.

hasPart.name: River to Coastal Pathway
hasPart.description: River discharge plus tides → estuarine salinity, sediment behavior, and coastal response.

hasPart.name: Snowmelt Habitat Pathway
hasPart.description: Snowpack → seasonal melt → watershed flow → wetland, river, and wildlife habitat response.

hasPart.name: Drought Pathway
hasPart.description: Drought → reduced infiltration and recharge → lower baseflow and wetland contraction.

hasPart.name: Extreme Storm Pathway
hasPart.description: Extreme storms → rapid runoff, flooding, erosion, sediment transport, and coastal response.
